Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the rectangular solid box $$B .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{B} ( 2 x + 3 y^{2} + 4 z^{3} ) d V ,$$ where $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le 2 , 0 \le z \le 3 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the rectangular solid box $$B .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{B} ( x y + y z + x z ) d V ,$$ where $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 1 \le x \le 2 , 0 \le y \le 2 , 1 \le z \le 3 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the rectangular solid box $$B .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{B} ( x \text{cos} y + z ) d V ,$$ where $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le \pi , -1 \le z \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the rectangular solid box $$B .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{B} ( z \text{sin} x + y^{2} ) d V ,$$ where $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le \pi , 0 \le y \le 1 , -1 \le z \le 2 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: $$\int_{0}^{1} \int_{1}^{2} \int_{2}^{3} ( x^{2} + \text{ln} y + z ) d x d y d z$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: $$\int_{0}^{1} \int_{-1}^{1} \int_{0}^{3} ( z e^{x} + 2 y ) d z d x d y$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: $$\int_{-1}^{2} \int_{1}^{3} \int_{0}^{4} ( x^{2} z + \frac{1}{y} ) d x d y d z$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: $$\int_{1}^{2} \int_{-2}^{-1} \int_{0}^{1} \frac{x + y}{z} d x d y d z$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: Let $$F , G , \text{and} H$$ be continuous functions on $$[ a , b ] , [ c , d ] ,$$ and $$[ e , f ] ,$$ respectively, where $$a , b , c , d , e , \text{and} f$$ are real numbers such that $$a < b , c < d , \text{and} e < f .$$ Show that $$\int_{a}^{b} \int_{c}^{d} \int_{e}^{f} F ( x ) G ( y ) H ( z ) d z d y d x = ( \int_{a}^{b} F ( x ) d x ) ( \int_{c}^{d} G ( y ) d y ) ( \int_{e}^{f} H ( z ) d z ) .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, change the order of integration by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$x ,$$ then $$y .$$
Question: Let $$F , G , \text{and} H$$ be differential functions on $$[ a , b ] , [ c , d ] ,$$ and $$[ e , f ] ,$$ respectively, where $$a , b , c , d , e , \text{and} f$$ are real numbers such that $$a < b , c < d , \text{and} e < f .$$ Show that $$\int_{a}^{b} \int_{c}^{d} \int_{e}^{f} F^{\prime} ( x ) G^{\prime} ( y ) H^{\prime} ( z ) d z d y d x = [ F ( b ) - F ( a ) ] [ G ( d ) - G ( c ) ] [ H ( f ) - H ( e ) ] .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | a \le x \le b , h_{1} ( x ) \le y \le h_{2} ( x ) , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( 2 x + 5 y + 7 z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le - x + 1 , 1 \le z \le 2 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | a \le x \le b , h_{1} ( x ) \le y \le h_{2} ( x ) , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( y \text{ln} x + z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 1 \le x \le e , 0 \le y \le \text{ln} x , 0 \le z \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | a \le x \le b , h_{1} ( x ) \le y \le h_{2} ( x ) , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( \text{sin} x + \text{sin} y ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le \frac{\pi}{2} , \text{unicode[:-]} \text{cos} x \le y \le \text{cos} x , -1 \le z \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | a \le x \le b , h_{1} ( x ) \le y \le h_{2} ( x ) , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x y + y z + x z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , \text{unicode[:-]} x^{2} \le y \le x^{2} , 0 \le z \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the indicated bounded region $$E .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x + 2 y z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le x , 0 \le z \le 5 - x - y \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the indicated bounded region $$E .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x^{3} + y^{3} + z^{3} ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 2 , 0 \le y \le 2 x , 0 \le z \le 4 - x - y \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the indicated bounded region $$E .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} y d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - 1 \le x \le 1 , \text{unicode[:-]} \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} \le y \le \sqrt{1 - x^{2}} , 0 \le z \le 1 - x^{2} - y^{2} \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the indicated bounded region $$E .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} x d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - 2 \le x \le 2 , - \sqrt{4 - x^{2}} \le y \le \sqrt{4 - x^{2}} , 0 \le z \le 4 - x^{2} - y^{2} \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E$$ of the form $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} x^{2} d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 1 - y^{2} \le x \le y^{2} - 1 , -1 \le y \le 1 , 1 \le z \le 2 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E$$ of the form $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( \text{sin} x + y ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - y^{4} \le x \le y^{4} , 0 \le y \le 2 , 0 \le z \le 4 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E$$ of the form $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x - y z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - y^{6} \le x \le \sqrt{y} , 0 \le y \le 1 , -1 \le z \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E$$ of the form $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , e \le z \le f \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} z d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 2 - 2 y \le x \le 2 + \sqrt{y} , 0 \le y \le 1 , 2 \le z \le 3 \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} z d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - y \le x \le y , 0 \le y \le 1 , 0 \le z \le 1 - x^{4} - y^{4} \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x z + 1 ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le \sqrt{y} , 0 \le y \le 2 , 0 \le z \le 1 - x^{2} - y^{2} \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x - z ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | - \sqrt{1 - y^{2}} \le x \le 0 , 0 \le y \le \frac{1}{2} , 0 \le z \le 1 - x^{2} - y^{2} \}$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, evaluate the triple integrals over the bounded region $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | g_{1} ( y ) \le x \le g_{2} ( y ) , c \le y \le d , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} .$$
Question: $$\iiint_{E} ( x + y ) d V ,$$ where $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le \sqrt{1 - y^{2}} , 0 \le y \le 1 , 0 \le z \le 1 - x \}$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: $$\iint_{D} ( \int_{1}^{2} ( x + z ) d z ) d A ,$$ where $$D = \{ ( x , y ) | x^{2} + y^{2} \le 1 \}$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: $$\iint_{D} ( \int_{1}^{3} x ( z + 1 ) d z ) d A ,$$ where $$D = \{ ( x , y ) | x^{2} - y^{2} \geq 1 , 1 \le x \le \sqrt{5} \}$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: $$\iint_{D} ( \int_{0}^{10 - x - y} ( x + 2 z ) d z ) d A ,$$ where $$D = \{ ( x , y ) | y \geq 0 , x \geq 0 , x + y \le 10 \}$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: $$\iint_{D} ( \int_{0}^{4 x^{2} + 4 y^{2}} y d z ) d A ,$$ where $$D = \{ ( x , y ) | x^{2} + y^{2} \le 4 , y \geq 1 , x \geq 0 \}$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: The solid $$E$$ bounded by $$y^{2} + z^{2} = 9 , z = 0 , x = 0 ,$$ and $$x = 5$$ is shown in the following figure. Evaluate the integral $$\iiint_{E} z d V$$ by integrating first with respect to $$z ,$$ then $$y , \text{and then} x .$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: The solid $$E$$ bounded by $$y = \sqrt{x} ,$$ $$x = 4 ,$$ $$y = 0 ,$$ $$z = - 2$$ , and $$z = 1$$ is given in the following figure. Evaluate the integral $$\iiint_{E} x y z d V$$ by integrating first with respect to $$x ,$$ then $$y ,$$ and then $$z .$$

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: [T] The volume of a solid $$E$$ is given by the integral $$\int_{-2}^{0} \int_{x}^{0} \int_{0}^{x^{2} + y^{2}} d z d y d x .$$ Use a computer algebra system (CAS) to graph $$E$$ and find its volume. Round your answer to two decimal places.

Problem Statement: $$E = \{ ( x , y , z ) | ( x , y ) \in D , u_{1} ( x , y ) \le z \le u_{2} ( x , y ) \} ,$$ where $$D$$ is the projection of $$E$$ onto the $$x y$$ -plane.
Question: [T] The volume of a solid $$E$$ is given by the integral $$\int_{-1}^{0} \int_{\text{unicode[:-]} x^{2}}^{0} \int_{0}^{1 + \sqrt{x^{2} + y^{2}}} d z d y d x .$$ Use a CAS to graph $$E$$ and find its volume $$V .$$ Round your answer to two decimal places.

Question: Consider the pyramid with the base in the $$x y$$ -plane of $$[ -2 , 2 ] \times [ -2 , 2 ]$$ and the vertex at the point $$( 0 , 0 , 8 ) .$$ Show that the equations of the planes of the lateral faces of the pyramid are $$4 y + z = 8 ,$$ $$4 y - z = -8 ,$$ $$4 x + z = 8 ,$$ and $$-4 x + z = 8 .$$ Find the volume of the pyramid.

Question: Consider the pyramid with the base in the $$x y$$ -plane of $$[ -3 , 3 ] \times [ -3 , 3 ]$$ and the vertex at the point $$( 0 , 0 , 9 ) .$$ Show that the equations of the planes of the side faces of the pyramid are $$3 x + z = 9 , - 3 x + z = 9 , - 3 y + z = 9 , \text{ and } 3 y + z = 9$$ Find the volume of the pyramid.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use two circular permutations of the variables $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ to write new integrals whose values equal the value of the original integral. A circular permutation of $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ is the arrangement of the numbers in one of the following orders: $$y , z , \text{and} x \text{or} z , x , \text{and} y .$$
Question: The midpoint rule for the triple integral $$\iiint_{B} f ( x , y , z ) d V$$ over the rectangular solid box $$B$$ is a generalization of the midpoint rule for double integrals. The region $$B$$ is divided into subboxes of equal sizes and the integral is approximated by the triple Riemann sum $$\sum_{i = 1}^{l} \sum_{j = 1}^{m} \sum_{k = 1}^{n} f ( \overset{-}{ \left ( x_{i} \right ) } , \overset{-}{ \left ( y_{j} \right ) } , \overset{-}{ \left ( z_{k} \right ) } ) \text{Delta} V ,$$ where $$( \overset{-}{ \left ( x_{i} \right ) } , \overset{-}{ \left ( y_{j} \right ) } , \overset{-}{ \left ( z_{k} \right ) } )$$ is the center of the box $$B_{i j k}$$ and $$\text{Delta} V$$ is the volume of each subbox. Apply the midpoint rule to approximate $$\iiint_{B} x^{2} d V$$ over the solid $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le 1 , 0 \le z \le 1 \}$$ by using a partition of eight cubes of equal size. Round your answer to three decimal places.

Question: [T] Apply the midpoint rule to approximate $$\iiint_{B} e^{\text{unicode[:-]} x^{2}} d V$$ over the solid $$B = \{ ( x , y , z ) | 0 \le x \le 1 , 0 \le y \le 1 , 0 \le z \le 1 \}$$ by using a partition of eight cubes of equal size. Round your answer to three decimal places. Use a CAS to improve the above integral approximation in the case of a partition of $$n^{3}$$ cubes of equal size, where $$n = 3 , 4 \text{,unicode[:ddot.],} 10 .$$

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use two circular permutations of the variables $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ to write new integrals whose values equal the value of the original integral. A circular permutation of $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ is the arrangement of the numbers in one of the following orders: $$y , z , \text{and} x \text{or} z , x , \text{and} y .$$
Question: Suppose that the temperature in degrees Celsius at a point $$( x , y , z )$$ of a solid $$E$$ bounded by the coordinate planes and $$x + y + z = 5$$ is $$T ( x , y , z ) = x z + 5 z + 10 .$$ Find the average temperature over the solid.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use two circular permutations of the variables $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ to write new integrals whose values equal the value of the original integral. A circular permutation of $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ is the arrangement of the numbers in one of the following orders: $$y , z , \text{and} x \text{or} z , x , \text{and} y .$$
Question: Suppose that the temperature in degrees Fahrenheit at a point $$( x , y , z )$$ of a solid $$E$$ bounded by the coordinate planes and $$x + y + z = 5$$ is $$T ( x , y , z ) = x + y + x y .$$ Find the average temperature over the solid.

A circular permutation of $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ is the arrangement of the numbers in one of the following orders: $$y , z , \text{and} x \text{or} z , x , \text{and} y .$$
Question: Show that the volume of a right square pyramid of height $$h$$ and side length $$a$$ is $$v = \frac{h a^{2}}{3}$$ by using triple integrals.

Question: Show that the volume of a regular right hexagonal prism of edge length $$a$$ is $$\frac{3 a^{3} \sqrt{3}}{2}$$ by using triple integrals.

Problem Statement: In the following exercises, use two circular permutations of the variables $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ to write new integrals whose values equal the value of the original integral. A circular permutation of $$x , y , \text{and} z$$ is the arrangement of the numbers in one of the following orders: $$y , z , \text{and} x \text{or} z , x , \text{and} y .$$
Question: If the charge density at an arbitrary point $$( x , y , z )$$ of a solid $$E$$ is given by the function $$\rho ( x , y , z ) ,$$ then the total charge inside the solid is defined as the triple integral $$\iiint_{E} \rho ( x , y , z ) d V .$$ Assume that the charge density of the solid $$E$$ enclosed by the paraboloids $$x = 5 - y^{2} - z^{2}$$ and $$x = y^{2} + z^{2} - 5$$ is equal to the distance from an arbitrary point of $$E$$ to the origin. Set up the integral that gives the total charge inside the solid $$E .$$
